key_insight: The paper makes real-time robust control tractable by pairing GPU-parallel linearization error bounds with LTV approximations, yielding formally certified robustness for nonlinear and neural-network dynamics.
engineering_relevance: It enables online synthesis of safe feedback policies and reachable tubes for high-dimensional systems while preserving both speed and formal constraint guarantees.
method: Path-based Hessian bounds for tighter analytic linearization error bounds than interval methods
Certified NN linearization bounds via verifier affine relaxations plus local Jacobian corrections
GPU-parallel SLS solver extended for right-invertible disturbance matrices and non-zero-centered zonotopic uncertainty
evidence_signal: The abstract reports GPU performance up to 67 Hz on systems with as many as 168 state dimensions, with reduced solve time and conservativeness versus baselines.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate verifier relaxations and solver compatibility with the disturbance/zonotope assumptions, so implementation details may affect tightness and scalability.
paper_title: GPU-Parallel Linearization Error Bounds for Real-Time Robust Optimal Control of Nonlinear and Neural Network Dynamics
